commits.subject: test(0.8.3/Slice 10 fix-1): RED — cost.n_errors reconciliation on retry-exhaustion
fix(0.8.3/Slice 10 fix-1): pyright-narrow dynamic answerer accessors + reconcile cost.n_errors (codex §9 P2+P3)

findings_addressed.how: Added `cast` to the typing import; narrowed the two dynamic getattr-callables via `cast(Any, fn)()` so `float(usd())` is type-safe and the cost_block result is built as an explicit `dict[str, Any]` (isinstance(Mapping) guard + dict() copy) before `.setdefault`/mutation. No `# type: ignore`, no gate-config change. pyright: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Replaced `cost_block.setdefault("n_errors", n_errors)` (which never overwrote the answerer's stale 0) with `cost_block["n_errors"] = max(n_errors, int(cost_block.get("n_errors", 0)))`, reconciling the answerer's own ledger with the run-level retry-exhausted count so the artifact's `cost.n_errors == answer_completeness.n_errors` (self-consistent ledger before any priced run). One-line comment documents the choice.

agent_verify_result: PASS — RED test reproduced the ledger inconsistency (cost.n_errors=0 vs answer_completeness.n_errors=4) on the retry-exhaustion path; GREEN fix makes both equal the forced-failure count; all gates (pyright, fix-test, -k suite, ruff) green; canonical repo clean of my edits.
